title: Wage Theory
focus: Distinguishes between Minimum Wage and Living Wage concepts and their constitutional/legal basis in India.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 058]
About Major Forms of Wages in India
Minimum Wage
- The wage must provide not only for the bare sustenance of life, but also for the preservation of the workers' efficiency.
- Payment of Minimum wages is legally mandated under Minimum Wages Act, 1948.
- Both Centre and States governments can fix, review, and revise minimum wages.
- Under the Code on Wages, 2019, minimum wages are applicable across all employments in public and private sectors, and organized and unorganized sectors.
Living Wage
- A living wage is a level of pay that gives workers and their families a decent standard of living.
- This includes being able to afford basic needs such as food, housing, healthcare, education, transport and clothing (ILO).
- Living wages are generally higher than minimum wages and should reflect regional differences.
- Article 43 (DPSP): The State shall endeavour to secure living wages to all workers by suitable legislation etc.
